title: Replace faulted bulk drive
problem: mpathv FAULTED; raidz2-2 down to one parity. Reclassified from Urgent to Next-window: bulk is near-empty (E-29) so this restores redundancy rather than averting data loss.
recommended_action: zpool replace bulk mpathv /dev/disk/by-id/<new>; scrub after
prerequisites: >=4TB SAS drive seated in one of the 2 free bays; drive is fully offline so confirm by-id at swap time
rollback: Pull new drive -> returns to current degraded (not worse)

title: Close the ZFS device-level alerting blind spot
problem: ZfsPoolDegraded could never fire: node_zfs_pool_health derives from 'zpool list -o health' which returns ONLINE for a raidz2 pool that still has redundancy. No per-device metric existed.
recommended_action: APPLIED: added node_zfs_pool_nonhealthy_devices{pool} (counts vdev devices not ONLINE) + critical ZfsDeviceFaulted rule (>0 for 5m, notify=infra); deployed to randy/quarkylab/jarvis
validation: Verified live in Prometheus: bulk=1, all other pools 0
rollback: Revert collector + remove rule (config-as-code branch fix/zfs-device-level-alert)

title: Pin the EX3400 as STP root
problem: No bridge-priority was set anywhere; the UniFi USW-24-250W had won root by lower MAC and the core reached it at cost 20000. Docs wrongly claimed the core was root at 4096.
recommended_action: APPLIED: set protocols rstp bridge-priority 4096, committed
validation: Verified: Root ID==Bridge ID==4096.c0:42:d0:f9:ec:71; ge-0/0/46 role ROOT->DESG
rollback: delete bridge-priority and commit (or let commit confirmed lapse)
